post_mortem: The thesis was structurally sound — two starters with low walk rates, a HR-suppressing dome, and a flat market line all pointed toward a low-event game. A 10-5 final (15 runs) represents a significant blowup that the underlying profile didn't predict, likely driven by a multi-run inning or early starter exit — exactly the tail risk flagged in the case against. The data was accurate and the reasoning was coherent; the outcome was simply in the bad tail.
sample_size_warning: N=1, insufficient — one high-scoring outlier in a dome game does not invalidate dome suppression factors or low-walk-rate pitcher theses.
